name: Green Corn Tamales
recipeIngredient: 1 Can evaporated milk, 13 oz. (1 and ½ cups)
recipeInstructions:
1. Husk the corn and save the inner leaves for wrapping the tamales.
2. Cut the corn from the ears and grind the kernels. If not ground fine enough, add milk and grind again (amount depends on corn quality).
3. Melt lard until it smokes, then set aside to cool.
4. Mix cooled lard, sugar, and salt into the ground corn. Taste and adjust sweetness or saltiness as needed. The mixture should be a smooth batter.
5. To assemble tamales: Place about 1 and ½ tablespoons towards the stem end of the husk. Add cheese and chili strips. Fold the sides of the husks over to cover the filling, then fold in half.
6. Stack folded side down to keep tamale closed until ready to cook. Makes about six dozen tamales.
7. To cook: Line the bottom of a large kettle with corn cobs and add water to the cobs. Spread discarded husks over the cobs, then stand tamales on top of the husks, open end up. Steam for 45 minutes. Open one tamale to sample and adjust cooking time if necessary.
